image.name: Image Dataset
image.description: CIFAR-100 image classification with CNN, ViT, and Ensemble models

results.summary: Image classification results on CIFAR-100 test dataset: Ensemble of ResNet50 and ViT-B/16 models

confusionMatrix.description: Confusion matrix for CIFAR-100 classification showing model performance across 100 classes
confusionMatrix.note: 100 classes across 20 supercategories (e.g., aquatic mammals, fish, flowers, vehicles, etc.)

observations: ViT-B/16 outperformed ResNet50 (81.27% vs 55.01% accuracy) due to better global context modeling
Ensemble of ResNet50 and ViT-B/16 achieved 81.19% accuracy, combining local texture and global attention strengths
ViT showed greater robustness to Gaussian noise (10% std) with only 2% accuracy drop compared to CNN's 5% drop
MobileNetV3-small struggled with CIFAR-100 (1.17% accuracy), highlighting challenges for lightweight architectures on fine-grained classification

reports.title: EDA Report
reports.subtitle: Problem and Dataset Analysis
items: CIFAR-100 dataset analysis: 60,000 32x32 color images in 100 classes across 20 supercategories
Perfect class balance with exactly 500 training images per class
Channel imbalance analysis: Blue channel darker, prevalence of white backgrounds
Spatial center bias identified through average image analysis

reports.title: Data Preparation Report
reports.subtitle: Dataset, DataLoader, and Augmentation Techniques
items: Image preprocessing: resize to 224x224, normalization with CIFAR-100 mean/std
Data augmentation: random horizontal flip (p=0.5), color jitter (brightness, contrast, saturation, hue)
Train/Test split: 50,000 training images, 10,000 test images
DataLoader configuration with batch size 128 and pin_memory for GPU acceleration

reports.title: Methodology Report
reports.subtitle: Construction, Training, Evaluation, and Comparison of CNN, ViT, and Ensemble Models
items: Model architectures: ResNet50 (CNN), ViT-B/16 (Vision Transformer), MobileNetV3-small (lightweight CNN)
Transfer learning: Pretrained ImageNet backbones frozen, only final classification head trained
Training: Adam optimizer (lr=0.001), cross-entropy loss, 5 epochs
Evaluation metrics: Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-Score (macro average)
Ensemble method: Soft voting averaging probabilities from ResNet50 and ViT-B/16

reports.title: Experimental Results
reports.subtitle: Tables, Charts, Analysis, and Discussion
items: Quantitative results comparison: ResNet50 (55.01%), ViT-B/16 (81.27%), MobileNetV3 (1.17%), Ensemble (81.19%)
Confusion matrix visualization for ViT-B/16 showing error patterns across 100 classes
Error analysis: Top 5 most confused class pairs (e.g., 'apple' vs 'pear', 'dolphin' vs 'whale')
Training curves: Loss and accuracy over epochs for all models
Robustness evaluation: Gaussian noise (10% std) impact on model performance

reports.title: Extension Reports
reports.subtitle: Additional Experiments/Improvements
items: Interpretability: Grad-CAM visualizations for ResNet50, attention maps for ViT-B/16
Robustness evaluation: Gaussian noise injection to test model robustness
Ensemble methods: Soft voting ensemble of ResNet50 and ViT-B/16
Efficiency analysis: Parameter count vs inference time vs accuracy trade-offs
Comparative analysis: ViT's superior performance despite higher computational cost

text.name: Text Dataset
text.description: DBpedia-14 text classification: BiLSTM (Frozen GloVe) vs BERT (Frozen Backbone)

results.summary: Text classification on DBpedia-14 (14 Wikipedia article categories, 14K train / 4.2K test): Comparing BiLSTM with frozen GloVe embeddings vs BERT with frozen backbone (linear probing)

confusionMatrix.description: Confusion matrices for BiLSTM (GloVe Frozen) and BERT (Backbone Frozen) on DBpedia-14
confusionMatrix.note: 14 classes: Company, EducationalInstitution, Artist, Athlete, OfficeHolder, MeanOfTransportation, Building, NaturalPlace, Village, Animal, Plant, Album, Film, WrittenWork

observations: BiLSTM (96.7%) slightly outperformed BERT (93.3%) — BiLSTM trains ~600K LSTM+FC params while BERT frozen trains only ~10K classifier params
Both models achieve >93% accuracy on DBpedia-14, showing the power of pretrained embeddings for topic classification
BERT backbone frozen = pure linear probing (99.9% params frozen), yet achieves 93.3% — strong representation quality
WrittenWork is BERT's weakest class (77%) — often confused with Album and Film due to similar creative work descriptions
Person-related categories (Artist, Athlete, OfficeHolder) show minor confusion in both models due to biographical text overlap

reports.title: EDA Report
reports.subtitle: Problem and Dataset Analysis
items: DBpedia-14 dataset: 14 Wikipedia article categories from HuggingFace (fancyzhx/dbpedia_14)
Subsampled: 1,000 train + 300 test per class (14,000 / 4,200 total), balanced across all classes
Text length distribution analysis: median word count and statistical summary
Class distribution plots confirming perfect balance across 14 categories
Word frequency analysis: top 20 most common words across all categories

reports.title: Data Preparation Report
reports.subtitle: Preprocessing, Tokenization, and DataLoader Setup
items: Text preprocessing: lowercase, remove URLs/HTML, remove special characters, normalize whitespace
BiLSTM tokenization: custom vocabulary (words with freq ≥ 2), pad/truncate to MAX_LEN=256
BERT tokenization: WordPiece tokenizer (bert-base-uncased), pad to MAX_LEN=256
GloVe embedding matrix: vocab words mapped to pretrained GloVe 6B 100d vectors
DataLoader: batch_size=64 (BiLSTM), batch_size=16 (BERT, higher memory usage)

reports.title: Methodology Report
reports.subtitle: BiLSTM (GloVe Frozen) vs BERT (Backbone Frozen) — Transfer Learning Comparison
items: Model 1: BiLSTM — Embedding(GloVe 100d, FROZEN) → Dropout → BiLSTM(2 layers, hidden=128) → Mean Pooling → FC(256,128) → FC(128,14)
Model 2: BERT — bert-base-uncased (12 layers, ALL FROZEN) → Linear(768, 14) classifier head only
BiLSTM training: Adam (lr=1e-3), StepLR (step=5, γ=0.5), gradient clipping (max_norm=1.0), 10 epochs
BERT training: Adam (lr=1e-2, higher LR for fresh linear layer), StepLR, 10 epochs
Key principle: Transfer Learning — frozen pretrained layers + only task-specific heads trained

reports.title: Experimental Results
reports.subtitle: Metrics, Confusion Matrices, Training Curves, and Comparison
items: Side-by-side comparison: Accuracy, F1, Precision, Recall for both models
Confusion matrix heatmaps: BiLSTM (Blues) and BERT (Greens) on 14 classes
Training curves: loss convergence and train/test accuracy over 10 epochs
Per-class accuracy comparison: which categories each model excels at
Parameter analysis: BiLSTM ~600K trainable vs BERT ~10K trainable (but BERT has 109M frozen)

reports.title: Extension Reports
reports.subtitle: t-SNE Visualization, Text Augmentation, Attention Analysis, Error Analysis
items: Extension 1: t-SNE/PCA visualization of BERT [CLS] embeddings showing semantic clustering before fine-tuning
Extension 2: Text data augmentation — synonym replacement (WordNet), random deletion, combined augmentation
Extension 3: BERT self-attention visualization — [CLS] attention weights and full attention heatmaps for 4 categories
Extension 4: Hard-negative error analysis — highest confidence errors, most confused class pairs (Artist↔Athlete, OfficeHolder↔Artist)

multimodal.name: Multimodal Dataset
multimodal.description: COCO multimodal classification: CLIP Zero-Shot vs Few-Shot Prototype Network (16-shot)

results.summary: Multimodal classification on COCO 2017 supercategories (~10 classes, ~6K samples): Zero-Shot (text prompts only) vs Few-Shot (16-shot Prototype Network with image+text), both using frozen CLIP (ViT-B/32)

confusionMatrix.description: Confusion matrices for Zero-Shot and Few-Shot (16-shot Prototype) classification on COCO supercategories
confusionMatrix.note: 10 COCO supercategories: animal, electronic, food, furniture, indoor, kitchen, outdoor, person, sports, vehicle

observations: Few-Shot (52.8%) outperformed Zero-Shot (35.5%) by +17.3% — using actual image+text features instead of generic text prompts
Zero-Shot excels at 'food' (84% accuracy) but fails on 'person' (10%) — generic prompt 'a photo of a person' is too ambiguous for COCO
Few-Shot dramatically improves 'animal' (72%), 'vehicle' (70%), and 'outdoor' (60%) through real prototype examples
Neither approach trains any neural network layers — both use frozen CLIP (151M params, 0 trainable)
Modality gap (L2=0.829) confirmed: image and text embeddings occupy separate regions in CLIP's shared space

reports.title: EDA Report
reports.subtitle: COCO Dataset and Supercategory Analysis
items: COCO 2017 Validation: 5,000 real-world images with object annotations and human-written captions
Labels from COCO supercategories (animal, vehicle, furniture, etc.) — top 10 with ≥100 samples selected
Class distribution visualization across supercategories
Caption length distribution analysis by category
Sample captions per category showing language diversity

reports.title: Data Preparation Report
reports.subtitle: CLIP Processing, Data Split, and Feature Extraction
items: Text preprocessing: lowercase, remove special characters, normalize whitespace
Image loading: local val2017 images (224×224 for CLIP ViT-B/32)
Data split: 20% support set (for few-shot prototypes), 80% test set
Few-shot support set: K=16 examples per class sampled from support split
CLIP processor handles tokenization (max 77 tokens) and image normalization

reports.title: Methodology Report
reports.subtitle: CLIP Architecture, Zero-Shot vs Few-Shot Prototype Network
items: CLIP (openai/clip-vit-base-patch32): dual-encoder (ViT image + Transformer text) trained on 400M image-text pairs, ALL FROZEN
Zero-Shot: encode class text prompts ('a photo of a [class]') → cosine similarity with image embeddings → softmax prediction
Few-Shot: extract multimodal features [image(512) || text(512)] = 1024-d → compute mean prototype per class → classify by nearest prototype
Key: Zero-Shot uses image-only features vs text prompts; Few-Shot uses multimodal concatenation vs class prototypes
No training, no optimizer, no loss function — pure frozen CLIP feature extraction + similarity computation

reports.title: Experimental Results
reports.subtitle: Metrics, Confusion Matrices, Per-Class Accuracy Comparison
items: Side-by-side comparison: Accuracy, F1, Precision, Recall for Zero-Shot vs Few-Shot
Confusion matrix heatmaps: Zero-Shot (Blues) and Few-Shot (Greens)
Per-class accuracy comparison showing which categories benefit from few-shot examples
Prototype similarity matrix visualizing inter-class relationships
Analysis: why Few-Shot outperforms Zero-Shot (real features vs generic text prompts)

reports.title: Extension Reports
reports.subtitle: Modality Gap, Patch Maps, Robustness, Error Analysis, Interactive Demo
items: Extension 1: Modality Gap Visualization — t-SNE of image vs text embeddings showing separate clusters in shared CLIP space
Extension 2: Cross-Modal Patch-Level Similarity Maps — 7×7 heatmaps showing WHERE CLIP focuses for each text query
Extension 3: Robustness Testing — Gaussian noise (σ=50), random crop (50%), word shuffle corruptions on zero-shot accuracy
Extension 4: Hard-Negative Error Analysis — highest confidence errors with hypothesis categories (text confusion, visual ambiguity, weak signal)
Extension 5: Interactive Gradio Demo — upload image + optional text caption → CLIP predicts COCO supercategory with similarity scores

technicalSummary.title: Technical Summary
techniques: Convolutional Neural Networks (ResNet50) and Vision Transformers (ViT-B/16) for image classification
Ensemble methods (soft voting) combining CNN and ViT predictions
BiLSTM with frozen GloVe 6B 100d embeddings for text classification (RNN-based)
BERT-base-uncased with frozen backbone for text classification (Transformer-based, linear probing)
CLIP (ViT-B/32) for multimodal zero-shot and few-shot classification
Prototype Networks (metric-based few-shot learning) with cosine similarity
Transfer Learning: frozen pretrained layers + task-specific heads
Data Augmentation: image (flip, jitter), text (synonym replacement, random deletion)
t-SNE / PCA for embedding visualization and modality gap analysis
Attention visualization (BERT self-attention, CLIP patch-level similarity)
technicalSummary.architecture:
Image: ResNet50 + ViT-B/16 → Ensemble (soft voting)
Text: BiLSTM(GloVe frozen) vs BERT(backbone frozen)
Multimodal: CLIP Zero-Shot (text prompts) vs Few-Shot Prototype (16-shot, concat fusion)

All approaches use Transfer Learning with frozen pretrained weights
